sections.paragraph:
φαίνω [Skt. assoc.]

—1. ‘function in a manner that makes observation possible’, w. focus on provision for lighted condition

—a. act. shine, appear J 1:5; 2 Pt 1:19; 1 J 2:8; Rv 1:16; 8:12; 18:23; 21:23. In imagery J 5:35.

—b. pass. in act. sense flash, of lightning Mt 24:27; shine, appear 2:7; Phil 2:15.

—2. ‘be in a state or condition of being visible or observed’, pass. intr. sense appear

—a. Mt 9:33; 13:26; 24:30; Hb 11:3; Js 4:14; 1 Pt 4:18.

—b.w. focus on arrival or presence Mt 1:20; Mk 16:9; Lk 9:8.

—c. w. focus on characteristic Mt 6:5; 23:27f; Lk 24:11; Ro 7:13; 2 Cor 13:7.

—3. ‘enter a state or condition that is capable of being grasped mentally or spiritually’, appear Mk 14:64.
